terms.definition: The derivative of a multivariable function with respect to one variable while all other variables are held constant.
Standard notation for the partial derivative of f with respect to x; also written f_x.
A higher-order partial such as ∂²f/∂x∂y, obtained by differentiating with respect to one variable and then another.
The first-order linear approximation to a surface z = f(x, y) at a point, built from partial derivatives ∂f/∂x and ∂f/∂y.
How much a function output changes locally when one input coordinate is perturbed; measured by the corresponding partial derivative.
